items.title: Source Coverage and Capability Model v0
items.user_value: Makes it explicit which source families can satisfy which query families before new fixtures or connectors are added.
items.implementation_feasibility: High; governance/model docs and validation can be added without runtime connector behavior.
items.regression_risk: Low if treated as schema/inventory planning only.
items.expected_audit_delta: No immediate query status change; future source packs become easier to target and test honestly.
items.suggested_milestone: Source Coverage and Capability Model v0
acceptance_criteria: source gaps map to needed fixture families
tests_to_add_first: placeholder source cannot claim implemented capability
search usefulness query family maps to source capability need
do_not_do: do not mark placeholder sources implemented
items.notes: Implemented as the source capability/coverage-depth model required before fixture expansion.

items.title: Real Source Coverage Pack v0
items.user_value: Adds recorded fixture evidence for old-platform software and starts turning source_gap results into partial/covered results when evidence supports it.
items.implementation_feasibility: Medium; requires curated recorded fixtures but no live network behavior.
items.regression_risk: Medium; fixture provenance and placeholder honesty need guards.
items.expected_audit_delta: Reduce selected old-platform source_gap counts and increase evidence-backed partial results.
acceptance_criteria: recorded fixtures are local and deterministic
source records distinguish active fixtures from placeholders
audit result changes are justified by evidence
do_not_do: do not call live source APIs
do not add broad source federation
do not weaken existing hard queries
items.notes: Implemented with tiny Internet Archive-like recorded metadata/file-list fixtures plus committed local bundle fixtures. It added no live API calls, scraping, crawling, broad source federation, or arbitrary local filesystem ingestion.

items.title: Old-Platform Software Planner Pack v0
items.user_value: Improves query interpretation for the selected wedge so source evidence can be routed toward the right object type and constraints.
items.implementation_feasibility: Medium; deterministic rules can be extended after source capability expectations are defined.
items.regression_risk: Medium; planner output changes affect goldens and eval checks.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: reduced planner_gap/query_interpretation_gap for old-platform queries while preserving source/capability gap honesty.
acceptance_criteria: apps are not confused with OS media
do_not_do: do not claim compatibility without evidence
items.notes: Implemented as deterministic Python-oracle planner behavior. Rust query-planner parity should target these refreshed Python goldens later.

items.title: Member-Level Synthetic Records v0
items.user_value: Lets Eureka show the smallest actionable member while preserving parent container lineage.
items.implementation_feasibility: Medium; can begin with local synthetic package/support-media fixtures.
items.regression_risk: Medium; member target refs and index records need stable shape.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: selected package/container queries now have bounded member target refs, lineage, and index/search visibility where fixture evidence exists.
do_not_do: do not add arbitrary local filesystem reads
do not broaden extraction formats beyond fixtures
items.notes: Implemented for bounded committed local bundle fixtures. Parent container demotion is now handled by Result Lanes + User-Cost Ranking v0.

items.title: Result Lanes + User-Cost Ranking v0
items.user_value: Clarifies why one result is more useful than another once evidence and source coverage exist.
items.implementation_feasibility: Implemented after source/member evidence became available.
items.regression_risk: High if it becomes hidden ranking or fuzzy relevance.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: current result records now expose deterministic lanes, user-cost scores, and reasons where available.
items.notes: Implemented as bounded deterministic usefulness annotations over current result records; no production ranking, fuzzy/vector retrieval, LLM scoring, or live source behavior was added.

items.title: Compatibility Evidence Pack v0
items.user_value: Makes old-platform answers actionable by citing compatibility evidence or preserving unknown compatibility honestly.
items.implementation_feasibility: Implemented with fixture-backed metadata, member paths, README text, and compatibility notes.
items.regression_risk: Medium; false compatibility claims are risky.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: current fixture-backed results now expose source-backed compatibility evidence where available while preserving unknown compatibility.
do_not_do: do not infer compatibility as truth
do not create a compatibility oracle
items.notes: Implemented as a bounded source-backed evidence seam. It does not create a compatibility oracle, execute installers, call live sources, scrape external systems, add fuzzy/vector retrieval, use LLM behavior, port Rust behavior, or add connectors.

items.title: Search Usefulness Audit Delta v0
items.user_value: Shows whether selected implementation slices actually improved audit outcomes without weakening fixtures.
items.implementation_feasibility: High now that source, planner, member, lane/user-cost, and compatibility evidence slices exist at v0 scope.
items.regression_risk: Low if it reports deltas honestly.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: partial count increased from 1 to 5, source_gap decreased from 43 to 41, and capability_gap decreased from 13 to 11 against the historical reported aggregate baseline.
do_not_do: do not rewrite queries to make deltas green
items.notes: Implemented as an audit/reporting pack under control/audits/search-usefulness-delta-v0. It records current Search Usefulness Audit results, historical baseline limitations, wedge-specific findings, and the next recommendation without changing retrieval behavior or recording external baseline observations.

items.title: Old-Platform Source Coverage Expansion v0
items.user_value: Adds the next recorded source material needed for old Windows/Mac utilities, drivers, support media, manuals, and latest-compatible release queries.
items.implementation_feasibility: Medium; should reuse the recorded-fixture approach from Real Source Coverage Pack v0 without adding live calls or arbitrary local ingestion.
items.regression_risk: Medium; status movement must stay source-backed and placeholder sources must remain honest.
items.expected_audit_delta: Implemented: current audit reports covered=5, partial=20, source_gap=28, capability_gap=9, and unknown=2 after evidence-backed fixture expansion.
acceptance_criteria: recorded fixtures are committed and deterministic
old-platform source gaps are targeted explicitly
external baselines remain pending unless manually recorded
audit deltas are rerun after fixture expansion
do_not_do: do not add broad source federation
items.notes: Implemented with expanded committed Internet-Archive-shaped records and three tiny local bundle ZIP fixtures. External baselines remain pending/manual and placeholder sources remain placeholders.

items.title: Search Usefulness Audit Delta v1
items.user_value: Records the measured movement after old-platform fixture expansion before the repo chooses another implementation slice.
items.implementation_feasibility: High; it should be audit/reporting and validation only.
items.regression_risk: Low if it does not change query fixtures or retrieval behavior.
items.expected_audit_delta: No new retrieval behavior; captures current covered=5, partial=20, source_gap=28, capability_gap=9, unknown=2 counts and query movement.
do_not_do: do not rewrite query fixtures to make deltas look better
do not fabricate Google or Internet Archive baselines
items.notes: Selected after Old-Platform Source Coverage Expansion v0 because the local audit changed substantially and should be recorded before the next implementation milestone.

items.title: Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0
items.user_value: Keeps the future Rust lane disciplined after Python planner behavior is useful enough to preserve.
items.regression_risk: Medium; parity drift can be subtle.
items.expected_audit_delta: No audit usefulness delta; improves migration confidence only.
items.suggested_milestone: Rust Query Planner Parity Candidate v0
acceptance_criteria: Rust output matches Python planner goldens
Cargo checks remain optional where needed
items.notes: Not immediate because usefulness gaps should be reduced in Python first.

items.title: Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0
items.user_value: Captures operator evidence for a supervised demo rehearsal without implying production hosting.
items.implementation_feasibility: High; templates and smoke checks already exist.
items.regression_risk: Low if no deployment behavior is added.
items.expected_audit_delta: No search usefulness delta; improves public-alpha confidence.
acceptance_criteria: smoke evidence is filled by operator
do_not_do: do not claim hosted alpha approval
items.notes: Useful but not the main usefulness wedge.

items.title: Compatibility Surface Strategy v0
items.user_value: Clarifies how compatibility evidence, unknowns, and actions should be presented once evidence exists.
items.implementation_feasibility: High as design/governance, medium as surface implementation later.
items.regression_risk: Low as docs, medium if UI behavior changes prematurely.
items.expected_audit_delta: No immediate delta; prepares UX/actionability improvements.
acceptance_criteria: user strategy does not mutate truth
do_not_do: do not build a modern app workbench
do not add native app shells
items.notes: Deferred until compatibility evidence pack defines what must be shown.

items.title: Manual Baseline Observation Workflow v0
items.user_value: Lets humans record Google/Archive observations later without scraping or fabrication.
items.implementation_feasibility: High; helper script already exists and can be hardened.
items.expected_audit_delta: External pending counts may decrease only when human evidence is committed.
tests_to_add_first: no observed baseline without evidence guard
do_not_do: do not call Internet Archive APIs
items.notes: Useful for comparison later, not a blocker for the next source/model work.

items.title: Internet Archive Recorded Fixture Connector v0
items.user_value: Would support article, manual, and item-file fixture evidence after capability model and source pack rules exist.
items.implementation_feasibility: Medium later; fixture provenance needs careful review.
items.regression_risk: Medium; easy to imply live IA behavior accidentally.
items.expected_audit_delta: Could reduce article/manual source gaps once recorded fixtures are added.
items.suggested_milestone: Internet Archive Recorded Fixture Connector v0
acceptance_criteria: no API calls in required tests
do_not_do: do not claim OCR coverage broadly
items.notes: Deferred until the next recorded-fixture expansion is scoped after compatibility evidence priorities are clearer.
